title: In monday CRM, what is the recommended or supported way to maintain accurate Contact-level lifetime revenue as multiple Deals are marked ‘Won,’ given the limitations around connected boards, mirrors, and automations?
excerpt: In monday CRM, what is the recommended or supported way to maintain accurate Contact-level lifetime revenue as multiple Deals are marked ‘Won,’ given the limitations around connected boards, mirrors, and automations?

posts.author: jsmplfd (Justin)
posts.cookedHtml: In monday CRM, what is the recommended or supported way to maintain accurate Contact-level lifetime revenue as multiple Deals are marked ‘Won,’ given the limitations around connected boards, mirrors, and automations?

posts.author: drtanvisachar (Dr. Tanvi Sachar- Certified Monday.com Partner)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hello @jsmplfd
In monday CRM, there isn’t a fully native way to automatically roll up Contact lifetime revenue from multiple Won Deals using only connected boards and mirrors. Mirror columns can display values but cannot conditionally sum Won deals and write totals back to a Contact column on their own.

Recommended approaches:

  1. Use Connected Boards + Mirror to display Deal values, then filter for Won deals and use a Formula column to calculate a visible total on the Contact board.

  2. Use an automation such as “When status changes to Won, update a number column in Contacts” to increment a Lifetime Revenue field. This is the most reliable native method.

  3. If you need more advanced rollups, use a Dashboard to aggregate Won revenue by Contact, or a marketplace app or integration tool to calculate and write totals back.

If you’d like hands-on help or want us to walk through this live, you can book a 1:1 paid 60-minute strategy session with our team here:

posts.author: Clover_Gal (Desiree McCarthy | monday.com Ambassador)
posts.cookedHtml:
There’s not a simple native way to do it from the board view on the Contacts board. That being said, what I do is create charts ‘inside’ the contact item card.
